data.title: Emerald Tyre Manufacturers IPO subscribed more than 100 times on day 2
data.description:
Emerald Tyre Manufacturers IPO

Emerald Tyre Manufacturers' IPO saw strong investor interest, with subscriptions reaching 102.71 times by December 6, primarily driven by retail and non-institutional investors. The bidding will close on December 9.

The Tamil Nadu-based tyre manufacturer targets Rs 49.26 crore through the public issue at the upper end of the price band of Rs 90-95 per share. The IPO consists of a fresh issuance of 49.86 lakh shares worth Rs 47.37 crore and an offer-for-sale of 1.99 lakh shares worth Rs 1.89 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Investors bought 38.22 crore equity shares compared to the offer size of 37.21 lakh shares via 2.54 lakh applications, the subscription data on the NSE showed.

Retail investors bid 165.99 times their reserved portion, and the part set aside for non-institutional investors was subscribed 133.56 times, while the response from qualified institutional buyers remained muted, buying 0.53 times the allotted quota.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

Emerald Tyre Manufacturers IPO shares attracted strong interest in the grey market, trading at nearly 80 percent premium over the upper price band, the market observers said. The grey market is an unofficial market for trading in IPO shares till the listing.

Emerald, which makes a wide range of tyres under the brand name GRECKSTER, is going to utilise fresh issue funds for its capital expenditure, general corporate purposes, and offer expenses.

The trading in Emerald Tyre shares will begin on the NSE Emerge effective December 12.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Nisus Finance Services IPO subscribed 177.55x on final day, grey market premium zooms ahead of listing next week
data.description:
Nisus Finance Services IPO

Nisus Finance Services closed its Rs 114-crore initial share sale with 177.55 times subscription on December 6 with support from across categories of investors.

Investors have applied for 80.9 crore equity shares during December 4-6, against the offer size of 45.56 lakh shares via more than 4 lakh applications, the BSE subscription data showed.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Non-institutional investors took the lead to support the offer subscription, bidding 324.43 times their reserved portion, followed by retail investors who bought 139.78 times the allotted quota. Qualified institutional buyers also boosted the IPO subscription, as the part set aside for them was subscribed 93.84 times, while the response was subdued by the company's employees who picked 90 percent shares of their book.

The public issue received a strong response from investors from the first day of bidding, getting 2.92 times subscriptions on December 4 and 18.81 times on December 5.

The IPO of the financial services provider consisted of a fresh issue of 56.45 lakh shares worth Rs 101.6 crore and an offer-for-sale of 7 lakh shares worth Rs 12.6 crore by the existing shareholders.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

The company that provides transaction advisory, and fund and asset management services are going to utilise net fresh issue funds for the India, Dubai and GIFT City-based funds, and invest in associate company Nisus Fincorp to increase its loan book.

The trading premium for Nisus Finance Services IPO shares in the grey market has increased further to 40-50 percent range from around 30 percent in the previous session, the market observers said. The grey market is an unofficial market wherein IPO shares can be bought and sold till the listing.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Investors can start trading in Nisus Finance Services shares on the BSE SME, effective December 11.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: DIIs net buy shares worth Rs 1,830 crore, FIIs net sell Rs 1,659 crore
data.description:
For the year so far, FIIs have net sold Rs 2.87 lakh crore, while DIIs have net bought Rs 5.76 lakh crore worth of shares.

Domestic institutional investors (DIIs) net bought shares worth Rs 1,830 crore, while on the other hand, foreign institutional investors (FIIs) sold shares worth Rs 1,659 crore, provisional data from NSE showed.

During the trading session, DIIs bought Rs 11,651 crore and sold shares worth Rs 9,992 crore, and FIIs purchased Rs 12,014 crore in shares while offloading equities worth Rs 13,844 crore.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

For the year so far, FIIs have net sold Rs 2.87 lakh crore, while DIIs have net bought Rs 5.76 lakh crore worth of shares.

Market View

At close, the Sensex was down 56.74 points or 0.07 percent at 81,709.12, and the Nifty was down 30.60 points or 0.12 percent at 24,677.80.

On the sectoral front, except IT and media, all other indices ended in the green.

Bajaj Auto, Axis Bank, SBI Life, Tata Motors, Maruti Suzuki are among top gainers on the Nifty, while losers were Adani Ports, Cipla, Bharti Airtel, HDFC Life and Asian Paints.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Siddhartha Khemka, Head - Research, Wealth Management, Motilal Oswal Financial Services Ltd notes that next week they expect Nifty to maintain its gradual upmove, driven by potential increase in liquidity post RBI’s CRR cut, positive news flows around government policies and return of FII inflows.

The Reserve Bank of India’s Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) decided to keep the benchmark repo rate unchanged at 6.5 percent for the 11th time in a row.

However, to improve liquidity in the banking system, the RBI Governor announced a reduction in the CRR to 4 percent, a cut of 50 bps.

data.title: Supreme Facility Management IPO to hit Dalal Street on December 11, price band at Rs 72-76 per share
data.description:
Supreme Facility Management IPO

Pune-based Supreme Facility Management is planning to raise Rs 50 crore through an initial public offering as it has filed a red herring prospectus with the Registrar of Companies on December 5.

The initial share sale, consisting of an entirely fresh issue of 65.79 lakh shares, will open for subscription on December 11, and close on December 13. The price band for the book-built issue has been fixed at Rs 72-76 per share.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The company offers integrated facility management services and support services including housekeeping and cleaning services, disinfecting and sanitizing services, pest control, horticulture, and facade cleaning, maintenance, repair, staffing, and corporate food solution services to corporates.

The facility management services provider will spend Rs 30 crore out of net fresh issue proceeds for working capital requirements, Rs 7.5 crore for inorganic initiatives, and the remainder for general corporate purposes.

It has reserved 10 percent IPO size for qualified institutional buyers, while the 45 percent portion each is reserved for retail and non-institutional investors.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

Investors can start trading in Supreme Facility Management shares on the NSE Emerge, effective December 18. Khandwala Securities is acting as the merchant banker on the issue.

With the strong equity market conditions, the IPO flow in the SME segment increased in December. Apart from Supreme Facility, Purple United Sales, Toss The Coin, Jungle Camps India, and Dhanlaxmi Crop Science public issues will also hit Dalal Street next week, while Nisus Finance Services IPO closed today, and Emerald Tyre Manufacturers will end the subscription next week.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: IPO-bound Vishal Mega Mart says discounted products helped retain customer base, fend off e-commerce rivals
data.description:
Vishal Mega Mart, backed by Kedara Capital has set the price band for its Rs 8,000-crore initial public offering (IPO) at Rs 74-78 per share. 

Retail chain Vishal Mega Mart has not lost any customers to online or e-commerce competitors despite the growing presence of new players in the market, its chief executive officer Gunender Kapur said.

He said Vishal Mega Mart operates a differentiated business model, offering products at significantly discounted prices—something online competitors may struggle to replicate.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

In the FMCG segment, more than 50 percent of Vishal Mega Mart's revenue comes from its in-house brands, which Kapur highlighted as a key differentiator.

He cited the example of the company’s chocolate cream biscuit, produced by the Australian multinational Magnetis. Vishal Mega Mart sells it at Rs 10.99 while other retailers offer similar products at Rs 36.

Similarly, while other retailers price 70-gram noodles at Rs 90, Magnetis noodles are available at Rs 60 at Vishal Mega Mart. "When customers compare, they see they are paying a significant premium for these products in other stores versus the prices we offer," he said.

Growth plans

Moving ahead, the company plans to grow its offline and online business rapidly, in terms of same-store sales growth and new store expansion, Kapur said.

While the online channel remains a focus, Kapur clarified that there are no plans to slow down the offline business. "We will give our best effort to grow both channels and strike the right balance," he added.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad
Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Avenue Supermarts, a competitor of Vishal Mega, Mart has seen clear disruption from online players and e-commerce retailers. Neville Noronha, CEO and MD of Avenue Supermarts, had said, "We clearly see the impact of online grocery formats, including DMart Ready, in large metro DMart stores which operate at a very high turnover per square feet of revenue."

Vishal Mega Mart, backed by Kedara Capital, has set the price band for its Rs 8,000-crore initial public offering at Rs 74-78 per share.

Vishal Mega Mart IPO will remain open for public subscription from December 11 to 13. The bidding for anchor investors is scheduled for December 10, a day before the offer opens for retail and institutional investors.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.

data.title: Suraksha Diagnostic makes subdued market debut, shares close 5% lower
data.description:
Suraksha Diagnostic makes subdued start, shares close 5% lower

  • Watchlist
  • Portfolio
  • Message
  • Set Alert
 
 
live
  • bselive
  • nselive
Volume
Todays L/H
More

Suraksha Diagnostic shares had a disappointing start on December 6, despite the strong equity market conditions, falling 5 percent at close.

The stock opened lower at Rs 438 on the NSE, down from issue price of Rs 441 and hit an intraday high of Rs 449 in opening trades, but immediately erased those gains and maintained downward move till the closing. It tested day's low of Rs 414.95 in late trade, before closing 5.4 percent down at Rs 417.25, with volume of 67.47 lakh equity shares.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

On the BSE, the stock finished at Rs 417.95, declining 5.23 percent with volume of 5.69 lakh shares.

Click Here To Read All IPO News

The Kolkata-based diagnostic services provider is valued at Rs 2,176.72 crore.

Suraksha Diagnostic launched its Rs 846-crore initial share sale last week, which was subscribed 1.27 times during November 29-December 3. The IPO comprised of solely offer-for-sale of 1.9 crore equity shares, while the price band for the offer was Rs 420-441 per share.

Hence, the entire IPO proceeds (excluding offer expenses) were received by the selling shareholders, while the company did not get any money from the issue.

The OrbiMed Asia II Mauritius-backed company provides pathology and radiology testing, and medical consultation services in West Bengal, Bihar, Assam, and Meghalaya states, having central reference laboratory, 8 satellite laboratories and 215 customer touchpoints which include 49 diagnostic centres, and 166 sample collection centres (primarily franchised).
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: SEBI proposes allowing use of stock data with a lag of 3 months for investor education
data.description:
If the name of any security or the market price data is used for the purpose of education, such market price data may be used with a lag of at least three months, the consultation paper said

A person who is doing only investor education should not use market price data of the preceding three months, the market regulator has proposed in a consultation paper issued on Friday.

The Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) has floated a consultation paper on December 6, seeking comments to clarify various points on how regulated entities such as stock brokers and exchanges can associate with unregistered entities.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

This would be a crucial point since many unregistered investment advisors claim that they are doing only investor education when discussing stocks and trading calls.

Also read: SEBI proposes allowing use of stock data with a lag of 3 months for investor education

In the consultation paper, the regulator has suggested that, for investor education no reference to data of a particular security should be made unless the data used is with a lag of three months.

The paper said, "A person engaged solely in education shall mean that such person is not engaged in any of the
wo prohibited activities, such person should not be using the market price data of the preceding three months to speak/talk/display the name of any security including using any code name of the security in his/her talk/speech, video, ticker, screen share etc. indicating the price, opinion, advice or recommendations related to security or securities. If the name of any security or the market price data is used for the purpose of education, such market price data may be used with a lag of at least three months."

(This copy will be updated.)
data.author: MoneyControl

data.description:

FMCG major Godrej Consumer Products is expecting sales growth in mid-single digit for the third quarter, and said demand conditions in the economy have been 'subdued' for the past few months, the company said on December 6.

"The standalone business is expected to report around flattish UVG (Underlying Volume Growth) and around mid-single digit sales growth in this quarter," GCPL said in a update on quarterly sales.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

"The demand conditions in India have been subdued for the past few months which is evident in FMCG market growth," said GCPL.

Despite this, the company has over the past six quarters 'consistently delivered
an average organic UVG of ~7%,' it added.

This is being updated.

data.title: CRR cut is the first step to monetary easing
data.description:
Quite interestingly the RBI has made an important announcement on having podcasts which is quite unique.

By Madan Sabnavis 

Banks can take heart from the credit policy unveiled today. The CRR cut was something which was required, as liquidity has been volatile in the last two weeks or so and would continue to be so in this month. The RBI has been using the liquidity framework of using variable rate repo (VRR) and variable rate reverse repo (VRRR) to stabilise liquidity. This has provided stability though banks wanted an assurance of more permanent liquidity. The CRR cut does this quite appropriately as the amount of Rs 1.16 lakh crore is large. This will have an impact on not just liquidity but also bond yields once the money flows in. Markets were expecting either a CRR cut or OMO purchases.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

First step to monetary easing

On deeper thought, the CRR cut can also be interpreted as a prelude to monetary easing. When the stance was changed to neutral in the last policy, it was more an articulation of possible easing with no specific action being taken. Now that liquidity has been increased, logically a rate cut can be expected going ahead provided inflation numbers turn favourable. Another view here is that this reduction has been timed when liquidity is tight or can get tighter; but it takes the rate back to what it was before the Ukraine war. Hence, it is not out of sync with the rate in the past.

The RBI has also raised the ceiling on interest rates on FCNR deposits. This is an interesting move for sure as it opens the door for more forex flows. But this will work in a big way provided banks increase their rates and customers find these rates attractive compared with other alternatives in their territories. Therefore, the efficacy of the same will depend on how banks calibrate their interest rates depending on the demand for such funds given that these deposits can be used for forex lending. Therefore, this would be something to be seen going ahead.

Podcasts add to communication channels

Quite interestingly the RBI has made an important announcement on having podcasts which is quite unique. It has to be seen as to how frequent would these be made and the subjects taken up as there are several things happening in the economy. This is in markets as well as economic indicators. Ideally if views are expressed on currency and liquidity conditions, they would be very useful signals for the market. Hence this will be something that everyone would look forward too. While the RBI does give forecasts on growth and inflation every time a policy is announced, a view on a more regular basis will provide valuable inputs.

The question now will be whether there can be a rate cut on the anvil? While the RBI will go based on data, some interesting scenarios can be spoken of. In December, it does look like that with inflation numbers of 6.2 percent and 5.5 percent in the preceding two months, it was difficult to pitch for a rate cut as inflation was high. In February, there would be information for November and December. Now Q3 forecast is 5.7 percent with one data point being 6.2 percent. The average for the next two numbers would be in the range of 5.4-5.5 percent and hence are likely to be above 5% in both the months. In this event, taking a call on rate cut will again be open to discussion.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

It does look like that economic conditions would be easing to a new normal in the next few months with growth stabilising at around 6.6 percent according to the RBI. Inflation too is to come down to 4.5 percent in the fourth quarter. A rate cut would be a signal to say that things can only get better from the point of view of industry. Given that the average repo rate in the last 10 years has been in the range of 6- 6.5 percent, the new cycle could be probably one with cuts of 25-75 basis points depending on how the inflation numbers behave.

(The author is Chief Economist, Bank of Baroda.)

Views are personal and do not represent the stand of this publication.
data.author: MoneyControl

data.title: Technical View: Momentum may drive Nifty above 25k post-RBI decision, Bank Nifty records biggest weekly gains since Sept
data.description:
Nifty Technical Trend

The Nifty 50, as expected, has seen consolidation throughout the session on December 6, falling moderately as the index seems to have already priced in the RBI move, rising nearly 1,000 points in the previous five consecutive sessions. The RBI slashed the cash reserve ratio by 50 bps to 4 percent, while raising the inflation forecast and lowering growth estimates for FY25. As long as the index holds 24,500, 24,720 (the middle of the Bollinger Bands) is the first target, followed by 24,900-25,000 as the crucial hurdle next week. Below 24,500, 24,350 is the support to watch, according to experts.

The Nifty 50 opened higher at 24,729 and remained in the range of 24,751-24,620 during the day before closing the session at 24,678, down 31 points. The index formed a small bearish candlestick pattern on the daily charts, but there was a long green candle on the weekly charts, with a continuation of higher highs and higher lows for the second straight week. The index rose 2.27 percent for the week, marking the biggest weekly gain since the first week of June.

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

Amol Athawale, VP of Technical Research at Kotak Securities, believes that the market texture is bullish. However, due to temporary overbought conditions, there could be rangebound activity in the near future. Therefore, the ideal strategy for short-term traders would be to buy on dips and sell on rallies, he advised.

For traders, according to him, key support levels would be 24,500 and 24,300, while resistance is expected to be between 24,900 and 25,050.

However, below 24,300, traders may prefer to exit from their long positions, he said.

On the Call side, the maximum open interest was seen at the 25,500 strike, followed by the 25,000 and 24,700 strikes, with maximum Call writing at the 25,000 strike, then the 24,700 and 25,500 strikes. On the Put side, the 24,000 strike holds the maximum open interest, followed by the 24,500 and 24,200 strikes, with maximum writing at the 24,200 strike, followed by the 24,700 and 24,000 strikes.

The weekly options data also indicated that 24,700 is likely to be the next resistance for the Nifty, followed by 25,000 as a key resistance, with support at 24,500.

Bank Nifty

Story continues below Advertisement
Remove Ad

The Bank Nifty also witnessed minor profit booking amid consolidation after five days of gains, declining 94 points to 53,510 and forming a bearish candlestick pattern with upper and lower shadows, resembling a High Wave candlestick pattern on the daily timeframe, indicating volatility. Hence, minor profit booking can't be ruled out, though the overall trend remains positive given the robust bullish candlestick pattern formation on the weekly timeframe. The banking index rallied 2.8 percent during the week, marking the biggest weekly gain since September.

Hence, "on the higher side, the index could attempt to test levels of 54,000-54,500 in the short term," Hrishikesh Yedve, AVP of Technical and Derivatives Research at Asit C Mehta Investment Intermediates, said.

On the downside, according to him, 52,850 will act as immediate support for the index, followed by 52,500. Thus, he advised traders to adopt a buy-on-dips strategy in the Bank Nifty.

After the passing of the RBI event, the volatility dropped, providing some comfort for bulls. The India VIX, the fear index, was down by 2.67 percent to the 14.14 level on Friday, while for the week, it fell by 1.98 percent.

Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by investment experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.
